items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Muthoot Housing Finance Company Limited
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated April 21, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹80,000/- (Rupees Eighty Thousand only) on Muthoot Housing Finance Company Limited (the company) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Fair Practice Code’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 52A of the National Housing Bank Act, 1987. The statutory inspection of the company was conducted by National Housing Bank with reference to its financial position as on March 31, 2025. Based on the supervisory findings

items.title: Reserve Bank of India imposes monetary penalty on Bandhan Bank Limited
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated April 21, 2026 imposed a monetary penalty of ₹41.80 lakh (Rupees Forty One Lakh Eighty Thousand only) on Bandhan Bank Limited (the bank) for contravention of the provisions of section 20(1)(b)(iii) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act) and non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on ‘Know Your Customer’. This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of section 47 A (1) (c) read with section 46 (4) of the BR Act. The Statutory Inspection for Supervisory Evaluation of the bank wa

items.title: Directions under Section 35A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 – The Irinjalakuda Town Co-operative Bank Ltd., Kerala – Extension of period
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India issued Directions under Section 35A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 to The Irinjalakuda Town Co-operative Bank Ltd., Kerala vide Directive No. CO.DOS.SED.No.D-01/12-22-350/2025-2026 dated July 29, 2025 for a period of six months up to close of business on January 30, 2026, which was extended up to close of business on April 30, 2026 vide Directive No. DOR.MON.No.D-53/12.26.061/2025-26 dated January 19, 2026. The Reserve Bank of India is satisfied that in the public interest, it is necessary to further extend the period of operation of the Dire

items.title: RBI approves the voluntary amalgamation of The Mattancherry Mahajanik Co-operative Urban Bank Ltd, Cochin, Kerala with The Peoples’ Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Tripunithura, Kerala
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India, in exercise of the powers conferred under sub-section (4) of Section 44A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of The Mattancherry Mahajanik Co-operative Urban Bank Ltd, Cochin, Kerala with The Peoples’ Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Tripunithura, Kerala. The Scheme will come into force with effect from April 27, 2026 (Monday). All the branches of The Mattancherry Mahajanik Co-operative Urban Bank Ltd, Cochin, Kerala, will function as branches of The Peoples’ Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Tripunithura, Kera

items.title: RBI cancels the Licence of Paytm Payments Bank Limited
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, vide order dated April 24, 2026, cancelled the banking licence issued to Paytm Payments Bank Limited under Section 22(4) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (‘BR Act’) effective from close of business on April 24, 2026. Consequently, Paytm Payments Bank Limited is prohibited from conducting the business of ‘banking’ as defined in Section 5(b) or any additional business specified under Section 6 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 with immediate effect. RBI will make an application for winding up of the bank before the High Court. Paytm Payments Bank Limited

items.title: Auction of 91-Day, 182-Day and 364-Day Treasury Bills
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India announces the auction of Government of India Treasury Bills as per the following details: Sr. No. Treasury Bill Notified Amount (₹ crore) Auction Date Settlement Date 1 91-Day 12,000 April 29, 2026 (Wednesday) April 30, 2026 (Thursday) 2 182-Day 6,000 3 364-Day 6,000 Total 24,000 The sale will be subject to the terms and conditions specified in the General Notification F.No.4(2)-B(W&M)/2018 dated March 26, 2025 , issued by Government of India, as amended from time to time. State Governments, Union Territories with legislature, eligible Provident Funds in India, designated

items.summary: The following State Governments have offered to sell stock by way of auction, for an aggregate amount of ₹14,500 Crore (Face Value). Sr. No. State Amount to be raised (₹ Crore) Tenor (Year) Type of Auction 1. Assam 1000 20 Yield 2. Bihar 1200 09 Yield 1200 Re-issue of 7.72% Bihar SGS 2041, issued on February 25, 2026 Price 3. items.title: China blocks Meta's $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus
items.summary: China said Monday it has decided to block Meta's $2 billion acquisition of Manus, a Singaporean AI startup with Chinese roots.

items.title: Brent oil prices briefly top $108 per barrel after Iran peace talks unravel
items.summary: President Donald Trump on Saturday cancelled plans to send U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Islamabad, Pakistan for negotiations with Iran.

items.title: India expects annual fertiliser subsidy bill to rise by 20%
items.summary: India's fertilizer subsidy bill is set to rise by 20% this financial year. This increase is driven by higher prices caused by the Middle East crisis. India is importing record amounts of urea at significantly increased costs. These rising import expenses will likely boost subsidies to companies selling fertilizers to farmers below market rates.

items.title: Global Market Update: Pakistan's central bank hikes key rate by 100 bps to 11.5%
items.summary: Pakistan's central bank ​raised its key policy ​rate by 100 basis points ​to 11.5% on Monday, its first hike in almost three years, as rising oil prices ‌from the Iran-U.S. ⁠war ⁠threatened to push inflation higher in the import-dependent ​nation.

items.title: IDFC First Bank deposit growth returning to 20%, credit costs falling, no West Asia impact yet: V Vaidyanathan
items.summary: IDFC First Bank's CEO V Vaidyanathan stated that the deliberate slowdown in deposit growth during Q4 was temporary and already reversing, with April data showing strong momentum. He expressed confidence in achieving 20% annual deposit growth in FY27, alongside lower credit costs and controlled operating expenses, indicating a robust financial outlook.

items.title: Cathie Wood sells $74-million shares of this semiconductor company after stock skyrockets 72% in one month
items.summary: Cathie Wood sold over $74 million in Advanced Micro Devices shares after a sharp rally, while buying Amazon stock. Despite outflows from ARK ETFs, she remains bullish on innovation and dismissed concerns about an AI-driven market bubble forming soon.

items.title: World leaders express shock, support after White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
items.summary: Leaders from the U.K., France, the EU and Pakistan react to a shooting at a high-profile dinner in Washington, D.C., with Trump and his cabinet members.

items.title: UK royals head to the U.S. in the wake of war, shooting and rocky relations
items.summary: The trip comes at a tricky time for the U.S. and one of its closest allies, the U.K.

items.title: United Airlines CEO confirms he approached American Airlines about merger
items.summary: United CEO Scott Kirby said American rejected the idea, "and without a willing partner, something this big simply can't get done."

items.title: MobiKwik shares jump 12% after RBI nod for NBFC licence to launch lending arm
items.summary: Shares of One MobiKwik Systems surged 12% on Monday after receiving the Reserve Bank of India's approval for an NBFC licence, enabling it to launch direct lending operations. The move marks a key step in its transition to a full-stack fintech platform, boosting growth and margin potential.

items.title: Amba Auto Sales & Services IPO Day 1: Issue booked 11% so far; check GMP, price band & other details
items.summary: Amba Auto Sales & Services IPO Day 1: The price band for Amba Auto Sales & Services IPO has been set at ₹130 to ₹135 per share. Retail investors need to apply for at least two lots of 1,000 shares each, resulting in a minimum payout of ₹135,000 at the upper end of the price band.

items.title: Oil could trade at nearly $120 if war drags on, Goldman Sachs warns
items.summary: Analysts raise base case for Brent to $90 a barrel in fourth quarter and see chance it could rise much higher

items.title: Axis Bank vs IndusInd vs IDFC First: Which bank stock should investors pick post Q4 results 2026?
items.summary: Post Q4FY26, Axis Bank emerges as the top pick for conservative investors due to its strong performance and asset quality, while IndusInd Bank is seen as a high-risk turnaround option. IDFC First Bank shows stable metrics but faces earnings visibility challenges.

items.title: MAHA is mad. Its alliance with Trump is about to face its biggest test
items.summary: Supreme Court arguments Monday and the farm bill put MAHA squarely at odds with President Donald Trump and the majority of Republicans in Congress.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki Q4 preview: Revenue to surge up to 30% YoY led by volumes, higher ASPs; brokerages mixed on PAT
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki India Limited is expected to post strong Q4FY26 revenue growth of up to 30% YoY, driven by higher volumes and improved realisations. However, brokerages remain divided on profitability, with estimates for net profit varying widely due to cost pressures and higher depreciation impacting margins.

items.title: Structural shift in Indian gold buy underway, 40% purchases in FY27 to be as investments: Report
items.summary: Indians are shifting their gold purchases towards investment. This trend is expected to continue, with investment demand potentially reaching 35-40% of total gold consumption in the current fiscal year. Gold ETFs and bar-and-coin buying are driving this surge. Despite higher prices, Indian jewellery demand remains strong, showing resilience.

items.title: Should private credit be stressing out insurers?
items.summary: Apparently not

items.title: Intellia Therapeutics says its Crispr-based treatment succeeds in pivotal trial
items.summary: Intellia Therapeutics said its Crispr-based treatment for hereditary angioedema met its goals in a Phase 3 trial, marking a milestone for gene editing.

items.title: Forget Vande Bharat, Shatabdi and Jan Shatabdi trains to get new seats, better toilets and smoother rides
items.summary: Shatabdi and Jan Shatabdi: Indian Railways has started a nationwide revamp of Shatabdi Express and Jan Shatabdi Express trains to significantly improve passenger comfort. The upgrade includes better seating, modern toilets, smoother ride quality, upgraded interiors and enhanced digital systems. Zonal railways have been asked to complete inspections and submit time-bound action plans.

items.title: Mahindra Holidays & Resorts India Q4 profit down 43 pc at Rs 41.49 cr
items.summary: Mahindra Holidays & Resorts India Ltd on Monday reported a 43 per cent decline in consolidated profit after tax to Rs 41.49 crore in the fourth quarter ended on March 31, 2026, impacted by outgo on impairment charges, new labour code and forex movement.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on April 27: Sun Pharma, Varun Beverages, Suzlon Energy, Anant Raj among top gainers
items.summary: On April 27, domestic equities rose nearly 1%, ending a three-day losing streak, driven by technology stocks and gains in pharma and consumer goods. The Nifty 50 and Sensex advanced 0.80%, with all sectoral indices closing higher.

items.title: Fed likely to hold rates steady as Powell prepares for possible swan song
items.summary: Federal Reserve policymakers will gather in Washington ​this week in what may be Jerome Powell's last meeting as head of the U.S. ​central bank, with energy prices still elevated and the Iran war at a standstill and likely to prolong uncertainty about the economic and monetary policy outlook.

items.title: India’s banking system turns sluggish amid loan recovery delays and legal hurdles: CII report
items.summary: India's banking sector needs urgent reforms. The Confederation of Indian Industry's report highlights slow loan recovery and complex laws. Reforms aim to speed up legal processes and improve efficiency. Stricter timelines and stronger institutions are recommended. Simplifying laws under a unified code is also proposed. These changes will strengthen financial discipline and boost investor confidence.

items.title: Rupee ends nearly flat, likely RBI presence blunts hit from higher oil prices
items.summary: The Indian rupee ended modestly stronger on ​Monday, as dollar sales by ​state-run banks cushioned the pressure from higher oil ​prices on the currency after the U.S.-Iran talks stalled.

items.title: Dr Nikhil Tondon appointed as officiating Director of AIIMS Delhi
items.summary: Dr Nikhil Tandon is now the officiating Director of AIIMS Delhi. He will hold this role for six months or until a permanent Director is chosen. This is a working arrangement. Dr M Sirinivas, the former Director, has moved to NITI Aayog. He replaces Dr VK Paul as a full-time member.

items.title: Office mkt shines in Jan-Mar despite global headwinds; net leasing up 7pc in top 7cities: JLL
items.summary: India's office market remained buoyant during January-March period of this year despite global uncertainties, with both gross and net leasing of workspaces rising by 10 per cent and 7 per cent, respectively, across seven major cities, according to JLL India.

items.title: L&T Realty Properties expands development portfolio with NCR land acquisition
items.summary: Infrastructure major Larsen & Toubro on Monday said one of its subsidiaries, L&T Realty Properties Ltd, has bought a 100 per cent stake in International Green Scapes Ltd (IGSL), unlocking 20 acres of prime land in Gurugram for real estate development.

items.title: Everything we know about Cole Allen, the suspected D.C. correspondents dinner gunman
items.summary: Allen was a guest at the Washington Hilton, where the dinner was held, and officials said he checked in on Friday.

items.title: Passenger Vehicle growth to slow to 4-6 % in FY27 on high base, macro risks; UVs to drive demand: ICRA
items.summary: Passenger vehicle industry growth in India is set to slow down in FY2027. This moderation follows strong performance in FY2026. Factors like a weak monsoon and global economic risks will be closely watched. Demand is expected to remain supported by GST rate cuts and new vehicle launches. Utility vehicles continue to lead sales. Exports also saw a significant increase.

items.title: Gujarat: VGRC to open new doors of opportunities in Surat's textile sector
items.summary: The Vibrant Gujarat Regional Conference will accelerate industrial growth in South Gujarat. This event aims to attract investments, connect global buyers with local manufacturers, and promote new technologies. Surat, a historic trade center, is now a global textile hub. The conference will further enhance its position by fostering foreign investment and technological exchange, strengthening its global competitiveness.

items.title: Explained | What’s inside India–New Zealand FTA: Tariff cuts, services push, farm safeguards & more
items.summary: Ind NZ FTA Agreement: India and New Zealand signed a significant free trade agreement. This pact opens doors for goods, services, and investment. It also allows more professionals to work in New Zealand. Sensitive sectors like dairy and agriculture are protected. This agreement is expected to boost bilateral trade substantially. It marks a new chapter in economic partnership.

items.title: ₹1,200 crore deal: Nvidia leases massive Bengaluru office space amid AI boom
items.summary: Nvidia, the world's most valuable company, has been steadily expanding its office footprint across cities. In Pune, Nvidia Graphics occupies around 3.75 lakh sq ft at Mindspace Business Parks REIT’s Commerzone property.

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: SBI Cards & TMB Q4 profit rise, UltraTech con. profit rises, ATGL, AU SFB, Coal India, Nippon Life to announce Q4 results
items.summary: Q4 Results Today, April 27, 2026, Live Updates: catch live updates

items.title: Stock Market Live Updates: Markets make further gains; Sensex jumps 703 pts to 77,367; Nifty spurts 209 pts to 24,107
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Live Apri 27: The benchmark indices BSE Sensex and NSE Nifty extended their afternoon gains ahead of the close on Monday. At 2.25 pm, Sensex was trading 77,367, up 703 points or 0.92 per cent. Nifty was at 24,107.00, up 209.05 points (0.87% higher).

items.title: Sterling Tools jumps 6% on ARAS deal with China’s Nanjing Haohang
items.summary: The stock climbed to ₹260 on the NSE by 2.32 PM, up ₹14.66 or 5.98 per cent from its previous close of ₹245.34, touching an intraday high of ₹268.99

items.title: Beyond the brew: Third Wave, Barista, Starbucks bet on desserts, diners, new experiences to lift margins
items.summary: Café operators are expanding food menus and exploring experiential formats to boost engagement and margins amid profitability pressures, but execution complexity remains a challenge.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty 50 snap 3-day losing run, end over 0.8% higher — 10 key highlights from the Indian stock market today
items.summary: Buying in Sun Pharma, index heavyweight Reliance Industries and select banking and IT counters drove Sensex and Nifty 50 higher even as the headwinds emanating from the US-Iran war lingered.

items.title: India's Peak power demand moderates to 238 GW on Sunday
items.summary: India's peak power demand dipped to 238.15 GW on Sunday, a decrease from Saturday's record 256 GW as commercial and industrial activities paused. Despite a slight shortfall, demand is nearing the government's summer estimate of 270 GW, driven by rising temperatures and increased use of cooling appliances.

items.title: Global Markets | European shares steady as investors eye US-Iran talks, central banks
items.summary: As investors steadied themselves for pivotal central bank meetings, European markets experienced minimal fluctuations. Oil prices rose, reflecting the stagnation in U.S.-Iran diplomatic progress. A downturn in technology and consumer staples stocks pulled the pan-European index downwards. On the upside, Germany’s DAX and Spain’s IBEX 35 enjoyed slight gains.

items.title: NBFC stock jumps 17% after the announcement of Q4 results 2026. Do you own?
items.summary: Aye Finance's shares rose 17% to ₹158 after strong March quarter results, with a 110% YoY profit increase. The company added over 70,000 borrowers and reported reduced credit costs, signaling a positive outlook for growth and risk management.

items.title: Oil rises as stalled US-Iran peace talks keep Hormuz Strait shut
items.summary: A ceasefire has mostly held since early April, but shipping blockades by both the US and Iran have cut daily transits through the Strait of Hormuz to near zero

items.title: Chhattisgarh boiler blast: Vedanta chief says operations and maintenance entrusted to NTPC-GE
items.summary: Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal has stated that NGSL, a joint venture between NTPC and GE, held full responsibility for the operations and maintenance of the Chhattisgarh power plant. An explosion at the plant on April 14 killed 25 workers. Agarwal drew an analogy to vehicle owners trusting drivers for safety.

items.title: Suzlon Energy rises 6.5% to 4-month high as Street spots multiple tailwinds; stock surges 45% in April
items.summary: Shares of Suzlon Energy rose 6.6% to ₹57.48, marking a four-month high amid aggressive buying. The stock has gained 45% in April, reversing a seven-month decline. Analysts cite geopolitical factors and growing demand for wind energy as key drivers of this momentum.

items.title: Petrol, diesel prices may rise ₹20 per litre in 3–6 months if crude stays above $100, says Emkay; lists stocks at risk
items.summary: India’s crude basket is currently trading around $110 per barrel. At these levels, under-recoveries for OMCs are estimated at approximately ₹18–20 per litre for petrol and diesel, even after the ₹10 per litre excise duty cut announced on March 27, 2026.

items.title: Varun Beverages doubles down on scale, Africa expansion drive
items.summary: Varun Beverages Ltd. is driving sales growth through capacity expansion and a strong focus on African markets. The company reported significant volume growth in India and international territories. Investments in manufacturing and distribution are key. Acquisitions in South Africa are strengthening its presence. Varun Beverages is confident in future growth due to favorable market conditions and its diversified portfolio.

items.title: Oil up almost 3% as US-Iran peace talks stall
items.summary: Oil prices jumped almost 3% on Monday as ​peace talks between the ​U.S. and Iran stalled and shipments through the Strait of ​Hormuz remained limited, keeping global oil supplies tight.

items.title: Nifty eyes 24,800 after healthy correction; Vinay Rajani picks Jindal Steel and NMDC
items.summary: Indian equities are showing renewed strength, with Nifty poised for further gains towards 24,800 after a healthy one-third retracement. Analysts highlight broad-based buying and a firm market structure. The steel and metals sector is particularly attractive, with Jindal Steel & Power and NMDC identified as promising investment opportunities for traders.

items.title: Aye Finance Q4 Results: Net profit jumps over 2-fold to Rs 86 crore
items.summary: NBFC Aye Finance on Monday reported a more than two-fold jump in net profit to Rs 86 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 31.

items.title: UltraTech Cement Q4 results: Net profit rises 21% to ₹3,011 crore; ₹240 special dividend announced
items.summary: UltraTech Cement Q4 results: The cement maker reported a 21.2% year-on-year (YoY) rise in its net profit, rising to ₹3,011 crore in the quarter ending on March 31, 2026, from ₹2,484 crore in the same period last year.

items.title: SBI Card Q4 Results: Net profit rises 14% YoY to Rs 609 crore, revenue up 6%
items.summary: SBI Card and Payment Services reported a 14% year-on-year increase in net profit to Rs 609 crore for the fourth quarter of FY26. Revenue from operations saw a 6% rise to Rs 4,934 crore during the same period. The company's shares experienced minor losses following the announcement of these financial results.

items.title: India’s office market defies global headwinds with record leasing in Q1
items.summary: With a record-breaking first quarter, India's office market is buzzing with optimism, achieving its highest leasing volume to date. This remarkable growth showcases a solid trust among occupiers, with significant contributions from tech giants, flexible office solutions, and the banking, financial services, and insurance sectors. Key cities like Bengaluru, Mumbai, and Hyderabad are at the forefront of this trend.

items.title: European markets edge higher as Iran reportedly makes peace proposal
items.summary: European stocks edged higher at the start of the new trading week as investors keep a close eye on stalled Iran-U.S. peace talks.

items.title: UltraTech Cement Q4 Results: Profit rises 20% YoY to Rs 2,983 crore; co declares Rs 240/share dividend
items.summary: UltraTech Cement Q4 Results: UltraTech Cement announced a significant 20% rise in its net profit for the fourth quarter. The company also recommended a special dividend of Rs 240 per share. This performance reflects a decade of strategic growth and acquisitions. Record profits and capacity expansion mark a successful financial year for UltraTech.

items.title: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri likely to visit Nepal next month: sources
items.summary: India's Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri is slated to visit Nepal in early May, marking the first high-level Indian delegation since the new government's formation. The visit aims to discuss bilateral relations and review India-funded projects, potentially paving the way for Prime Minister Balendra Shah's future trip to India.

items.title: Supreme Industries posts 46% jump in Q4 profit to ₹382 cr
items.summary: Revenue for the quarter came in at ₹3,536 crore, up 16.3%, driven by surge in plastic goods volumes to 2.32 lakh metric tonnes

items.title: Former Apollo risk chief says some new life insurers could struggle in downturn
items.summary: Chak Raghunathan cites heavy reliance on untested private credit and newer savings products that are vulnerable to withdrawals

items.title: Sun Pharma: How a small Kolkata trader became a global buyout artist
items.summary: Sun Pharma, led by Dilip Shanghvi, is poised for its largest overseas acquisition, a $12.5 billion deal for Organon & Co. This move continues Shanghvi's proven strategy of acquiring companies to fuel growth and global expansion. His approach prioritizes strategic advantage and turnaround potential, as seen in past landmark deals like Ranbaxy and Taro.

items.title: Sun Pharma deal structurally strong, debt the only overhang: Amit Khurana
items.summary: Market expert Amit Khurana offers insights on key corporate and regulatory developments. Sun Pharma's acquisition is seen as strategically sound, though debt requires careful management. Paytm Payments Bank's license cancellation has limited impact on its core business. Banks show stable earnings with upside potential, while NBFCs face stretched valuations. Consumption and select sectors present opportunities.

items.title: Global stocks steady; oil climbs on stalled peace talks
items.summary: Benchmark Brent crude futures rose almost 3% to touch a more than three-week high of $108.5 a barrel at one point in the session, stoking inflation worries

items.title: NSE sells 1% stake in Indian Gas Exchange to comply with regulatory norm
items.summary: The stake sale is part of NSE's effort to align with the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board’s norms

items.title: Tata Steel, JSW Steel or Jindal Stainless? Jefferies, Goldman Sachs bullish on these steel stocks
items.summary: International brokerages Jefferies and Goldman Sachs have issued bullish notes for the steel sector, citing multiple reasons for strong upside potential for JSW Steel and other key players in the field.

items.title: Eternal Q4 Preview: Will over 400% YoY profit growth help dominate Swiggy?
items.summary: Zomato is likely to report strong Q4 growth, with revenue rising about 180% YoY and profit up nearly 430%, driven by Blinkit’s continued momentum and operating leverage. The core food delivery business is expected to remain steady, with NOV growth of 17–18% and margins improving slightly, supported by better cost control and higher take rates.

items.title: Mobikwik share price skyrockets 16% after RBI approval for NBFC application
items.summary: Mobikwik share price rallied over 16% in Monday's trading session as investors cheered the receipt of an NBFC application from the RBI. The stock had listed in the Indian stock market in December 2024.

items.title: Small-cap stock edges higher after announcement of Q4 results 2026
items.summary: MIC Electronics shares rose 3% after announcing Q4FY26 results, showing a consolidated net loss of ₹18.35 crore. Despite this, revenue increased 13.3% to ₹51 crore, and EBITDA surged 60.6% to ₹13 crore, reflecting strong operational performance.

items.title: FPOs hold promise for smallholders, but face structural constraints: Study
items.summary: Farmer producer organisations (FPOs) are key to integrating smallholders in India, but they are constrained by scale, market access, finance, and governance gaps, says a recent policy paper.

items.title: Amazon Now expands to Pune, Hyderabad, Chennai, Kolkata as part of 100-city push
items.summary: Amazon is bringing its rapid delivery service, Amazon Now, to 100 cities across India. This expansion will be backed by over 1,000 specialised delivery hubs. The service offers daily essentials delivered within minutes. This move is part of a significant investment to boost Amazon's operations in India.

items.title: Tanla Platforms stock jump 19% as Q4 results boost sentiment
items.summary: The company reported a consolidated net profit of ₹134.32 crore for the quarter ended March 2026, marking a 14.4% y-o-y increase from ₹117.33 crore in the corresponding quarter last year

items.title: Varun Beverages Q1CY26 profit jumps 20% to ₹878 crore; stock rallies 2.2%
items.summary: Net revenue rose 18.1% to ₹6574.2 crore; gross margins expanded 62 bps as early raw material sourcing boosts profitability

items.title: Paytm Payments Bank licence cancellation: Jefferies, Bernstein and Goldman decode impact
items.summary: Paytm shares fell 8% after RBI cancelled Paytm Payments Bank’s licence but recovered sharply as brokerages stayed bullish. Analysts see limited financial impact, citing separation from PPBL and strong core business momentum. Jefferies, Goldman and Bernstein retained positive ratings, though risks to sentiment remain. Technical indicators suggest near-term volatility, with key support around Rs 1,050 and resistance near Rs 1,150.

items.title: Aye Finance posts 110% jump in Q4 profit; stock hits 52-week high
items.summary: Gross NPAs came in at 4.8% and net NPAs at 1.8%, down 17 basis points and 19 basis points, respectively, from the previous quarter

items.title: “No Bull, no bear—it’s a kangaroo market," says Nilesh Shah amid global uncertainty
items.summary: Indian markets show a recovery but face a "kangaroo market" due to global risks like West Asian tensions and oil price volatility. Investors are urged to be selective, focusing on adaptable companies, especially in IT and consumer goods, as AI disruption reshapes industries. The real economy anticipates pain as oil costs and supply chain disruptions impact consumers and businesses.

items.title: $250 bn e-commerce boom redraws India's retail buying & triggers a safeguard demand
items.summary: India's e-commerce sector is poised for massive growth, projected at $250 billion. Gen Z shoppers are driving this expansion, favoring new models like live and creator-led commerce. However, a traders' body warns that unregulated growth could harm traditional retail. They are urging the government for a clear e-commerce policy to ensure fair competition and protect jobs.

items.title: Europe’s rearmament push drives global military spending to record $2.9 trillion despite U.S. pullback
items.summary: Global military spending rose for an 11th straight year to a record $2.89 trillion in 2025, driven by Europe’s rearmament push, even as U.S. outlays declined.

items.title: India tech giants struggle to shake off $115 billion rout
items.summary: India's software services exporters face growth concerns after Infosys and HCL Technologies reported disappointing earnings. The IT sector gauge dropped over 5%, highlighting challenges from a weak global economy and AI disruption.

items.title: Hindustan Zinc shares: Jefferies, HSBC to Nuvama — why are brokerages bullish on Vedanta group stock? Explained
items.summary: Hindustan Zinc shares rose 9% on April 27 after strong Q4FY26 results, with a 67.6% net profit increase. The company also declared an interim dividend of ₹11, achieving record production levels and improving cost efficiencies, driving positive broker outlooks.

items.title: Bitcoin prices edge towards $80,000 amid US-Iran uncertainty. What should investors do?
items.summary: Bitcoin, the world’s largest cryptocurrency, rose as much as 1.6% to $79,488—its highest level since January 31. Here's what experts say about its near-term outlook.

items.title: TRAI seeks stakeholder input to boost public Wi-Fi expansion across India
items.summary: Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) on Monday released a consultation paper on the "Proliferation of Public Wi-Fi Networks in India", inviting stakeholder comments on ways to accelerate deployment, improve viability, and enhance user adoption across the country.

items.title: Listed developers dominate India's real estate land deals, consolidation trend continues
items.summary: Listed real estate developers are increasingly dominating land acquisitions, securing nearly half of all deals. This consolidation is driven by easier access to capital and transparent operations, giving them an edge over smaller players. Bengaluru leads in acquisition activity, while NCR homebuyers show a strong preference for trusted, institutional developers.

items.title: MobiKwik gets RBI nod for NBFC licence, to launch in-house lending arm
items.summary: Fintech firm MobiKwik has secured a crucial Reserve Bank of India approval for a non-banking financial company license. This paves the way for MobiKwik to launch its own lending business through a wholly owned subsidiary. The company plans to offer new credit products for consumers and merchants.

items.title: Adisoft Technologies IPO Day 3: Issue subscribed 28.69x so far. Check GMP, issue details, more
items.summary: Adisoft Technologies IPO started on April 23 and ends on April 27, with shares priced between ₹163 and ₹172. The company offers digital automation solutions and aims to raise capital for expansion, amid significant investor interest and a current GMP of +11.

items.title: India New Zealand FTA to help reduce India's dependence on select markets, says CITI
items.summary: India and New Zealand sign a Free Trade Agreement today. This pact is set to reduce India's reliance on specific markets. Indian textile and apparel exporters anticipate stronger efforts to move up the value chain. The agreement offers duty-free access for Indian textiles. New Zealand's premium wool imports will support high-end garment exports.

items.title: Lighter metal, heavier wallets: has the aluminium rally hit its melting point?
items.summary: Investors in aluminium stocks have seen significant gains, with companies like National Aluminium Co and Hindalco nearing all-time highs. A supply crunch amid geopolitical tensions has driven prices up, though analysts warn of potential downturns due to increased production and interest rates.

items.title: Sun Pharma surges 7%, Sensex tops 77,200 at noon; Shriram Finance, Axis Bank drag
items.summary: The pharmaceutical sector has emerged as a key defensive anchor through a week of broad-based selling, and Monday’s move amplified that trend decisively

items.title: Penny stock continues hitting upper circuit after 1:10 stock split, OFS, bonus issue announcement
items.summary: String Metaverse last week announced the successful completion of its offer for sale (OFS) of equity shares. The company also executed a stock split during the same period. It’s board of directors will also consider a bonus issue of shares on April 29.

items.title: Rising input costs continue to pressure Reliance’s O2C business: Yogesh Patil
items.summary: Reliance Industries navigates tough market conditions. Refining margins face volatility due to rising costs. Global crack spreads show improvement, but structural issues persist. The company's consumer businesses present a mixed outlook. Retail revenue grows healthily, but margins are impacted by online segment expansion. Telecom also shows growth. Analysts provide insights into these ongoing trends.

items.title: JSW Motors plans to open own showrooms before India car debut
items.summary: JSW Motors is preparing to launch company-owned showrooms across India ahead of its first vehicle debut later this year. The conglomerate aims to establish its new brand by opening at least four "experience centers" in key cities, offering customers a curated interaction with its upscale plug-in hybrid model.

items.title: Paytm shares slide nearly 4% as payments bank winds up; Goldman holds ‘Buy’
items.summary: Goldman Sachs, in a note published this morning, maintained ‘Buy’ rating on the stock with a revised 12-month price target of ₹1,400

items.title: Purple ube’s viral rise is turning a Filipino staple into a global trend — but supplies are tightening
items.summary: Ube, a naturally sweet, starchy vegetable grown in the Philippines, has transcended Filipino culture and become a social media sensation.

items.title: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei closes above 60,000 for first time on earnings optimism
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei index soared past the 60,000 mark for the first time, driven by strong corporate earnings and AI sector optimism, despite Middle East tensions. Keyence and Fanuc led the charge with significant gains. Investors are now keenly watching upcoming earnings reports, with potential profit-taking anticipated around the psychological 60,000 level.

items.title: Global Markets | Australian shares fall for fifth straight session as stalled US-Iran talks cap risk appetite
items.summary: Australian shares extended their decline for a fifth consecutive session, closing modestly lower as stalled U.S.-Iran peace talks weighed on risk appetite. Financials led the losses, with investors anticipating softer earnings due to higher rates and cost pressures. Miners were the only sector to advance, buoyed by a weaker U.S. dollar supporting gold.

items.title: Japan, South Korea stocks hit record high as investors shrug off stalled U.S.-Iran negotiations
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets climbed Monday as investors looked past renewed diplomatic setbacks between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: Basmati rice exporters urge govt action on shipping charges amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: Basmati rice exporters face crippling shipping charges. War-risk surcharges are soaring, sometimes reaching 70 percent of cargo value. Shipping lines are unilaterally diverting cargo and holding containers. Exporters bear these costs without recourse. The Basmati Rice Farmers & Exporters Development Forum demands government intervention to regulate these charges and ensure fair practices for maritime trade.

items.title: Sun Pharma acquires Organon: growth engine or debt trap?
items.summary: For Sun Pharma, Organon acquisition strengthens its pivot toward global branded and specialty therapies. If integration falters, balance sheet could become story.

items.title: ACKO taps merchant bankers for IPO; targets $2-2.5 billion valuation
items.summary: The IPO is likely to comprise a mix of fresh issue of shares and an offer-for-sale (OFS) by existing investors.

items.title: Elon Musk vs Sam Altman trial begins today: What's at stake, key allegations, and all you need to know
items.summary: Elon Musk's trial against OpenAI begins today in Oakland, California today. Musk accuses OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission for profit and is seeking $134 billion in damages.

items.title: India inks 'once-in-a-generation' free trade agreement with New Zealand after a decade of talks
items.summary: India-New Zealand FTA: India and New Zealand have inked a landmark free trade agreement, a "once-in-a-generation" pact opening new avenues for trade and investment. This deal, concluding years of negotiations, grants India duty-free access for numerous exports and boosts services, while New Zealand gains immediate access to over half its exports to India.

items.title: OnEMI Technology Solutions' ₹926 crore IPO to open on April 30
items.summary: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of equity shares aggregating to ₹850 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of 44,39,788 equity shares worth ₹76 crore at the upper end by existing shareholders

items.title: FIIs, retail investors raise stakes in 10 smallcaps; stocks rally up to 105% in CY26
items.summary: Analysis of BSE smallcaps shows rising FII and retail ownership in 164 stocks; despite broad declines, ten outperformed, delivering strong gains up to 105 percent in CY26.

items.title: The Chinese sports brand taking on Nike and Adidas
items.summary: Now one of the biggest sportswear firms, Anta's rise follows a playbook adopted by many Chinese giants.

items.title: We spoke to over 30 CEOs and business leaders. Here's what worries them most
items.summary: Business leaders across the world are confronting a new operating reality: one where war, inflation and artificial intelligence are part of the baseline.

items.title: IT stocks wipe off ₹767,000 crore m-cap YTD: Should you be greedy or fearful amid the ongoing selloff?
items.summary: Hurt by weak demand, tariff impact and H1-B visa fee hike in 2025, the headwinds for IT sector have only multiplied this year, with the growing shift to artificial intelligence (AI) emerging as a key risk, along with weak client spending and longer decision cycles.

items.title: Paytm Payments Bank is finished. Ashvin Parekh on what it means for your money & your Paytm app
items.summary: The RBI has cancelled Paytm Payments Bank's license. This formal step concludes a process that began in 2022. The winding-down process is now underway. However, your Paytm app and its payment services are a separate entity and will continue to function. This is because they operate under a different license. Depositors and wallet users will be repaid.

items.title: Turbulence ahead! UBS downgrades IndiGo stock, cuts target to ₹4,940, say reports; here's why
items.summary: InterGlobe Aviation shares rose over 1% to ₹4,571 despite UBS downgrading the stock to neutral and lowering its target price. UBS cited rising jet fuel prices and demand fatigue as concerns, although IndiGo remains better positioned than peers due to strong liquidity and scale.

items.title: Varun Beverages Q1 Results: Cons profit jumps 20% YoY to Rs 879 crore; revenue rises 18%
items.summary: Varun Beverages Q1 Results: Varun Beverages, a Pepsi bottler, announced a strong performance for the March quarter. The company's net profit saw a significant 20% year-on-year increase. Alongside these results, Varun Beverages declared an interim dividend for the fiscal year 2026. The record date for this dividend has been set for May 1, 2026.

items.title: Structural demand, liquidity discipline to support real estate outlook
items.summary: India's real estate sector is entering a period of normal growth. Developers must manage liquidity and sales to maintain financial strength. Demand is consolidating, with growth rates expected to slow. The mass market and upper-mid segments show resilience. Commercial real estate will see steady growth, driven by leasing activity. Geopolitical uncertainty may cause temporary delays.

items.title: Bank of Japan interest rate decision on Tuesday: Can BoJ hike short-term rates? How can it impact Indian stock market?
items.summary: As the Bank of Japan prepares to announce its interest rate decision, the potential implications of the BoJ's policies amidst global tensions, particularly in West Asia, create uncertainty for investors.

items.title: Gujarat Cotex to raise ₹43 cr via rights issue
items.summary: The rights issue comprising 8,54,64,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value ₹5 each, will close for subscription on May 8

items.title: Sun Pharma shares jump 7% as India's largest drugmaker to buy U.S. firm Organon in $11.75 billion deal
items.summary: To fuel its global ambitions, Sun Pharmaceutical Industries will acquire New Jersey-based Organon & Co in an all-cash deal that values the U.S. company at $11.75 billion.

items.title: RBL Bank shares drop 5% despite 3x jump in Q4 net profit. What's spooking investors?
items.summary: RBL Bank shares fell despite a strong Q4FY26, where net profit tripled to Rs 230 crore. The decline came as margins weakened, with NIM slipping to a five-quarter low, even as NII and operating profit posted moderate growth.

items.title: Less bread and WFH Fridays – what Pret boss knows about our habits
items.summary: Customers want great value but that doesn't necessarily mean the cheapest products, says Pano Christou.

items.title: Varun Beverages Q1 results: Net profit jumps 20% to ₹872 crore, revenue up 18% YoY; ₹0.50 dividend declared
items.summary: Varun Beverages Q1 results: Varun Beverages reported a Q1 net profit of ₹872.3 crore, up 20% YoY, with revenues rising 18.3% YoY to ₹6,721 crore. An interim dividend of ₹0.50 per share was announced, and robust demand supported strong performance in both domestic and international markets.

items.title: Markets have moved on from geopolitics, broader rally underway: Anurag Singh
items.summary: Investor sentiment is stabilizing, shifting focus from dominant tech stocks to broader market participation. Anurag Singh of Ansid Capital highlights market resilience despite geopolitical tensions, noting a move beyond Iran concerns and a potential shift away from the 'Magnificent Seven' towards a more diversified rally.

items.title: Multibagger stock Transformers and Rectifiers zooms over 10% after receipt of ₹150 crore order
items.summary: Transformers and Rectifiers shares rose over 10% following a $16.25 million export order win. The company reported a dip in profits but plans significant capital expenditure to increase capacity. Its current order book stands at ₹5,005 crore, reflecting strong international demand.

items.title: Acko lines up ICICI Sec, Kotak, Morgan Stanley for $2 billion IPO
items.summary: Bengaluru-based insurtech Acko has appointed ICICI Securities, Kotak Mahindra Capital and Morgan Stanley for an IPO that could value it at up to $2 billion.

items.title: India’s seaplane era set to begin as SkyHop gets nod for first commercial flights
items.summary: India's first dedicated commercial seaplane operator, SkyHop Aviation, has secured its Air Operator Certificate from the DGCA, marking a significant step towards launching operations. Following successful test flights, the airline plans to connect Lakshadweep islands and the mainland with a 19-seater aircraft, aiming to improve regional accessibility.

items.title: Coal India Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: PSU stock falls ahead of earnings, dividend today. What to expect?
items.summary: Coal India Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: Coal India is expected to report a fall in its net profit and revenue, driven by lower coal offtake. The company’s coal offtake in Q4FY26 is estimated to decline by 0.8% to 199 MT from 201 MT in the year-ago period. Sequentially, offtake is seen growing by 6%.

items.title: IDBI Bank sets board meeting for Q4 results 2026. Details here
items.summary: IDBI Bank said on Monday, 27 April, that will board of directors will meet on Thursday, 30 April to announce financial results for the quarter ending on March 31, 2026.

items.summary: The company has maintained its price target of ₹1,350, implying an 18% upside

items.title: Mapping the fallout: Which Indian sectors face risks from the West Asia war?
items.summary: The West Asia war has triggered an energy crisis, hitting sectors like fertilizers and airlines. With the Strait of Hormuz disrupted, rising costs and supply shortages could directly or indirectly impact nearly 30 sectors.

items.title: Rise, fall, repeat: Why Indian stock market volatility may persist and a sustained rally may remain elusive
items.summary: The Indian stock market experiences wild fluctuations driven by global cues, crude oil prices, and geopolitical tensions. Despite short-term gains, persistent uncertainties suggest a challenging outlook ahead.

items.title: Amid a rights issue offer, penny stock doubles shareholders' money in one month. Check details
items.summary: Gujarat Cotex's share price has surged over 100% in a month following a rights issue announcement to raise ₹42.73 crore for expansion. The issue offers shares at ₹5, significantly below the market price, and will close on May 8, 2026.

items.title: India–New Zealand to sign FTA to boost trade, investments and market access across goods and services
items.summary: India and New Zealand are set to sign a Free Trade Agreement. This pact will boost bilateral trade in goods and services. It will also encourage mutual investments. India gains duty-free access for all exports. New Zealand gets duty-free access for over 54% of its exports. The agreement deepens economic ties and strengthens India's Indo-Pacific trade strategy.

items.title: Crude oil prices rise 2.5% on US-Iran peace talks uncertainty; Goldman Sachs raises Brent Q4 forecast to $90
items.summary: Crude oil prices surged nearly 2.5% on Monday as the peace talks between the US and Iran stalled, and the Strait of Hormuz remained effectively shut. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs has raised its target for Brent and WTI crude for the fourth quarter.

items.summary: The share price decline despite a sharp rise in fourth quarter earnings highlights investor concerns around asset quality and margin trends

items.title: Robotic joint replacement surgery and insurance: The gap patients should know about
items.summary: Robotic surgery offers better outcomes for knee and hip pain patients. However, many Indians cannot access this advanced treatment due to insurance limitations. Insurers should update policies to cover robotic procedures equally with traditional ones. This will improve patient recovery and reduce long-term healthcare costs. Aligning insurance with medical progress ensures better healthcare for everyone.

items.title: Eveready Industries’ Rs 200-crore manufacturing plant to pave the way for global play
items.summary: Eveready Industries has launched a new Rs 200 crore manufacturing plant in Jammu. This facility is India's only operating alkaline battery plant. It will significantly increase production capacity. The plant aims to meet growing demand for high-performance power solutions. It will also reduce India's reliance on imported batteries. This move is set to boost local employment and supply chain resilience.

items.summary: Bharti Airtel has shown remarkable consistency and resilience over nearly four decades. The company has navigated regulatory challenges and intense competition. Airtel holds a significant market share and leads in average revenue per user. Its revenue and net profit have seen substantial growth. This journey highlights Airtel's successful business strategies and commitment to its customers and people.

items.title: ET Awards: Cyrus Poonawalla reflects on SII’s mission of affordable vaccines and saving lives worldwide
items.summary: Cyrus Poonawalla began Serum Institute of India in 1966 with a goal to make India self-reliant in healthcare. His vision was to provide affordable vaccines and save lives. The institute has since become a global leader, supplying vaccines to over 100 countries. It played a crucial role during the Covid-19 pandemic. Poonawalla hopes his son Adar will continue this mission.

items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman is preparing new reforms to ease business compliance. She believes strong domestic consumption will help India overcome global challenges. The government is also addressing cybersecurity threats from advanced AI models. Sitharaman assures that reforms will continue to support industry growth and manufacturing.

items.title: Sun Pharma to buy Organon in $11.75 billion all-cash deal
items.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries has agreed to acquire U.S. drugmaker Organon & Co in an all-cash deal valued at approximately $11.75 billion, including debt. The agreement stipulates Sun Pharma will purchase all outstanding Organon shares for $14.00 per share. This acquisition will grant Sun Pharma full ownership of Organon, a company focused on women's health, biosimilars, and established medicines.

items.summary: The government is seeking to address critical gaps in fire safety infrastructure, amid acute shortages of fire stations, equipment and personnel, raising concerns over response readiness as urban fire risks rise.

items.summary: The top motorcycle manufacturers are planning for premium bikes to capture double-digit growth and higher margins. This shift serves as a strategic buffer against a slowing entry-level market hampered by rural inflation and geopolitical volatility.

items.title: Essentials, staples drive Reliance's FMCG business, Qcomm push hits retail margins
items.summary: Reliance's consumer goods arm is seeing strong growth. Daily essentials are now its biggest revenue source, bringing in ₹8,800 crore in FY26. Beverages also performed well. The company's overall FMCG business is projected to reach ₹22,000 crore in FY26. Campa soft drinks lead the brand sales, with Independence staples following closely. Reliance is expanding its product range across various categories.

items.title: Paradise Biryani plans ₹100-crore fundraise
items.summary: Paradise Biryani plans to raise ₹100 crore. This funding will fuel aggressive expansion. The iconic Hyderabadi brand aims to open 100 new outlets in the next three years. This growth will extend its presence to major cities like Pune and Kolkata. Paradise currently operates 57 stores and expects to reach 160 outlets. The biryani market is experiencing strong growth.

items.summary: The soaring cost of driving presents a political problem for Trump ahead of midterm elections in November

items.title: CP Milk & Food Products in early talks to sell up to 15% at ₹3,000 crore valuation
items.summary: CP Milk’s fundraising efforts come against the backdrop of several family-owned businesses seeking to tap private markets to catalyze their next phase of growth

items.title: India's wheat production resilient despite setbacks: Agri ministry
items.summary: India's wheat output for the 2025-26 crop year is stable. The agriculture ministry assures resilience despite unseasonal rains and hailstorms. Increased acreage and better seed varieties are offsetting localized damage. Procurement data from key states shows healthy production. The overall scenario remains strong and steady.

items.title: The great fashion reset: Myntra, Nykaa, Tata Cliq and others go luxe
items.summary: Online fashion and beauty platforms are trying to shed their discount-driven identity by adding a layer of luxury.

items.title: Iran war hits pistachio supplies amid Dubai chocolate boom
items.summary: Prices for the nuts have surged after disruption to export routes from Iran, a major producer of the nut

items.title: FTA with New Zealand to provide huge opportunities for Agra's leather exporters: Goyal
items.summary: India and New Zealand are set to sign a free trade agreement on April 27. This pact will significantly boost bilateral trade. Indian companies, especially leather exporters from Agra, will gain duty-free access to New Zealand markets. The agreement aims to double trade and attract substantial investment over fifteen years.

items.title: India following diversified strategy to boost exports to China, cut import dependence: Official
items.summary: India is pursuing a multi-pronged approach to boost its exports to China. The nation is focusing on building its own industrial capabilities and reducing reliance on Chinese imports. This strategy aims to create resilient supply chains and enhance India's export capacity.

items.title: Hormuz disruption, $100 oil pose risks to India's inflation, rupee: Union Bank
items.summary: The West Asia conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruptions pose significant risks to India's economy, driving crude oil prices above $100/bbl. This is pressuring inflation, the rupee, and the current account, with the Indian rupee already sliding to record lows. The RBI is intervening to stabilize markets amid these elevated geopolitical uncertainties.

items.title: How one of the world’s smallest countries is feeling the fuel crisis
items.summary: Energy crunch triggered by Middle East war reaches Pacific island of Tuvalu

items.title: Nissan Is Betting on ‘China Speed’ to Get Back on Its Feet
items.summary: Nissan Motor Co. is counting on China to reverse its fortunes after headwinds in the US and Japan left the carmaker searching for a way to regain its footing.

items.title: India plans global diplomatic push across Europe, Africa, BRICS and Quad in May
items.summary: India is set to ramp up its global diplomacy in May, with multiple high-level engagements across Europe, Africa and key groupings such as BRICS and the Quad, aimed at advancing a multipolar world order. Narendra Modi is expected to visit Europe for the India-Nordic Summit and bilateral talks in Italy and the Netherlands, while India will host the fourth Africa Summit on May 31, with several African leaders likely to attend.

items.title: PJSC MGKL becomes first Russian publicly listed co to get global scale issuer rating in India
items.summary: PJSC MGKL, Russia’s first publicly listed resale operator, has received a ‘CareEdge B-/Stable’ global long-term issuer rating from CareEdge Global IFSC Limited, becoming the first Russian firm to be rated in India.

items.title: The FTA cautionary tale: What India-Korea trade warns us about new global deals
items.summary: In their bilateral trade pact, South Korea has gained significantly more than India. As it enters more such pacts, India would do well to review where it fell short with South Korea and why.

items.title: Avataar Venture Partners expects three IPOs from first two funds by 2028
items.summary: The growth-stage investor is pivoting toward deeptech and AI for its $400 million third fund, even as it targets a string of public listings to return capital from its first vehicle.

items.title: Tell us what you need to invest more: FM Sitharaman to India Inc
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman pledged government support for farmers facing rising input costs. She urged Indian industry to expand and add capacity, promising a patient hearing for their needs. The minister also discussed capital outflows and India's readiness for reforms. Engagement with the US and Anthropic is ongoing regarding AI model risks.

items.title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman pushes unified KYC framework, asks Sebi to lead
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman called for a single know-your-customer system across the financial sector. She asked the Securities and Exchange Board of India to lead this initiative. The minister also urged Sebi to focus on future risks. These include artificial intelligence misuse and cyber threats. A strong defense against cyberattacks is crucial for market stability and public trust.

items.title: ET Awards 2025 | Fundamentals sound but money markets work their way: FM Sitharaman on FDI outflows
Kedaara picks up 5% in Axis Finance for Rs750 crore, sets benchmark for IPO-headed NBFC
items.summary: Axis Finance has raised Rs750 crore from Kedaara Capital for a 5.08% stake, marking its first external funding. This capital boost strengthens its financial base as it jostles in India's extremely competitive credit market—preparing for a future IPO under regulatory requirements.

items.title: IDFC First Bank says ₹590 crore fraud impact behind it, sees strong FY27 growth
items.summary: The lender said new business remained unaffected during the March quarter, though profit fell sequentially after it fully expensed the fraud hit and incurred higher costs.

items.title: Transition at Sonata: Rajsekhar Datta Roy is new CEO as Samir Dhir steps down
items.summary: Sonata haild Roy for being instrumental in guiding the company's AI-first evolution. He has led efforts in advancing a Responsible AI approach, the company said.

items.title: Axis Bank frontloads FY27 provisions amid West Asia war uncertainty
items.summary: The private-sector lender set aside a one-time ₹2,001 crore buffer in Q4, citing macro and geopolitical risks, even as higher provisions weigh on profit and margins face near-term pressure.

items.title: India's financial system and external sector remain resilient despite global volatility and capital outflows: RBI Bulletin
items.summary: The RBI noted that key external sector vulnerability indicators "remained contained" and foreign exchange reserves continued to remain "comfortable" even amid heightened global uncertainty.

items.title: From car and phone to tractor owners, a populist wave is rising to end the 'captive' repair economy
items.summary: "Right to repair" legislation is an issue that political candidates across the U.S. have made part of their affordability messaging.

items.title: $300 bags, $150 earrings, $60 hats: Mid-priced products are a status symbol for young shoppers
items.summary: A growing number of Gen Zers and millennial consumers with disposable income are shifting away from both luxury- and discount-priced retail items alike.

items.title: 36-year-old left the U.S. for China—now pays $1,000 rent and $100 for groceries for family of 4: It's my 'version of the American Dream’
items.summary: A decade ago, Bradley Krae left the United States to teach English in Shenzhen, China. He spends less money and says the has found his own version of the American Dream there.

items.title: AI talent war: Software industry is a new target as top executives jump ship to OpenAI
items.summary: Software giants are seeing their worst stock performance in years on fears of AI disruption. Now they have a new problem.
